title: Mali Defence Minister Sadio Camara Confirmed Killed by JNIM Suicide Car Bomb — Wife and Grandchildren Also Dead
detail: Mali's Defence Minister General Sadio Camara (born March 22, 1979) was confirmed killed on April 26, 2026, one of the most senior government officials to die in the Sahel insurgency. A JNIM suicide car bomb struck his residence in Kati — the garrison town 15 km northwest of Bamako — in the early hours of April 25, 2026. His second wife and two grandchildren were also killed in the blast. Camara's death was publicly confirmed by multiple sources on April 26 after his fate had been listed as unknown in initial reporting. Camara was a central figure in the Malian security establishment: he participated in the 2020 coup that brought General Assimi Goïta to power, and had served as Defence Minister since then, overseeing the expulsion of French forces, the invitation of the Wagner Group/Africa Corps, and the November 2023 Kidal offensive. The Africa Report characterized his death as dealing 'a heavy blow to Bamako's military junta,' noting that he had been one of the most trusted and capable figures around Goïta. Junta leader Assimi Goïta was confirmed to be 'alive and well in a secure location' and 'remains in command of the military.' JNIM specifically claimed to have targeted Camara's residence as part of its April 25 coordinated offensive, alongside attacks on the home of junta leader Goïta, Bamako's Modibo Keïta International Airport, and other military targets.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Mali's Defence Minister Sadio Camara killed
The Africa Report — Death of Sadio Camara deals heavy blow to junta
Sahara Reporters — Mali Defence Minister killed in coordinated jihadist attacks

media.caption: Mali's Defence Minister General Sadio Camara confirmed killed by JNIM suicide car bomb at his Kati residence on April 25, confirmed April 26, 2026

title: Russia's Africa Corps Withdraws from Kidal Under FLA Escort — Kidal Falls Completely to Tuareg-Jihadist Alliance
detail: On April 26, 2026, Russia's Africa Corps and Malian Armed Forces soldiers who had been besieged at Camp 2 (the former MINUSMA camp) in Kidal since the April 25 JNIM-FLA offensive agreed to withdraw under escort by the Front de Libération de l'Azawad (FLA). According to France 24 and multiple corroborating sources, a convoy assembled at the entrance to the former MINUSMA camp and departed under armed FLA rebel escort — the same forces that had seized the city. The FLA subsequently declared Kidal 'totally' under its control, with videos circulating online showing armed FLA fighters inside the National Youth Camp of Kidal, the governor's residence, and other government facilities. This represents the largest single territorial loss for the Mali junta and its Russian partners since Africa Corps deployed in December 2021: Kidal — the city the junta had captured in November 2023 after an 11-year Tuareg absence — was lost in less than 24 hours. Russia-aligned media complained that the Africa Corps' role in defending Bamako was being deliberately ignored by Western outlets, noting Russian mercenary helicopters had patrolled Bamako's airport area during the April 25 attacks. The Kidal withdrawal removes the FAMa-Africa Corps presence from northern Mali's most strategically symbolic city. The FLA's alliance with JNIM — the first publicly confirmed coordinated military operation between Tuareg separatists and the al-Qaeda affiliate — creates a unified rebel-jihadist front controlling the Kidal-Gao corridor and effectively severing the Mali state's presence in the Azawad region. Analysts note this development reverses the single most-cited success of the Russia-Mali partnership.

sources.name: France 24 — Tuareg rebels announce deal for Africa Corps withdrawal from Kidal
Turkiye Today — Russia's Africa Corps withdraws from Kidal under rebel escort
Pravda France — Afrika Korps role in repelling Mali attack

media.caption: Africa Corps and FAMa forces withdraw from Kidal under FLA escort on April 26, 2026 — the largest territorial loss for the Mali junta-Russia axis since Africa Corps arrived in 2021

title: Bamako Under 3-Day Curfew; UN, ECOWAS, AU, US Condemn Mali Attacks; FAMa Claims Hundreds Killed in Repulsed Assault
detail: On April 26, 2026, the Malian government and the international community issued formal responses to the April 25 coordinated JNIM-FLA offensive. Mali imposed a three-day overnight curfew on Bamako and surrounding areas, as FAMa launched large-scale sweep operations throughout Kati, Bamako, and adjacent zones. Government spokesperson Issa Ousmane Coulibaly confirmed 16 wounded (civilians and military) and stated that 'several militants' had been killed; FAMa's official statement claimed 'several hundred assailants neutralized' — a figure not independently verified given media restrictions. Russian military personnel were noted as participating in the defense of Bamako, with three Africa Corps helicopters confirmed patrolling the Modibo Keïta International Airport zone. Internationally, UN Secretary-General António Guterres issued a statement saying he was 'deeply concerned by reports of attacks in several locations across Mali,' strongly condemned the violence, and called for 'coordinated international support to address the evolving threat of violent extremism and terrorism in the Sahel.' ECOWAS condemned the attacks and called on 'all states, security forces, regional mechanisms and populations of West Africa to unite and mobilize in a coordinated effort.' The African Union Chairperson strongly condemned the attacks, citing risks to civilian populations. The US State Department Bureau of African Affairs condemned the attacks and extended condolences. No statement from the French government was found, though France 24 characterized the events as 'a dramatic setback for the Malian government.' JNIM formally claimed responsibility for the joint operation via SITE Intelligence Group monitoring, stating: 'Together, we are carrying out a veritable transformation, in the service of religion, of the country and of the people.'

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Mali rattled by ongoing armed attacks: What to know
US News / Reuters (via SITE Intelligence) — JNIM claims Mali attacks with FLA
Africanews — Coordinated attacks rock Mali, injure 16

media.caption: UN, ECOWAS, AU and US condemn coordinated JNIM-FLA attacks across Mali — Bamako placed under 3-day overnight curfew, April 26, 2026
